Title: Multi-level engineering facilitates the production of phenylpropanoid compounds in tomato

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mutant locus deficient in flavanone 3-hydroxylase activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Genistein/genistin are phytoestrogenic bioactives; flavonols are antioxidant phytonutrients
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; crossing with E8:AtMYB12 and E8:LjIFS; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Acts through reduced F3H activity/competition with LjIFS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The are mutant is deficient in F3H activity. 2. In AtMYB12 background, are reduces flavonol accumulation to about 30% of AtMYB12 levels. 3. In LjIFS + AtMYB12 background, are strongly redirects flux to isoflavones, raising total isoflavones to about 78 mg g−1 DW.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces flavonol branch flux and thereby enhances engineered isoflavone accumulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful flux-rerouting allele for high-isoflavone tomato engineering when combined with AtMYB12 and IFS
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural tomato mutant used to reduce competition for naringenin

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glycolysis linked to shikimate/phenylpropanoid precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive polyphenols with nutritional and health-promoting properties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in AtMYB12 fruit; elevated in peel of WT fruit
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter; indirectly studied under E8:AtMYB12 background
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlENO transcript is strongly induced in AtMYB12 tomato fruit. 2. AtMYB12 and endogenous SlMYB12 bind the SlENO promoter directly. 3. SlENO activation is linked to increased glycolytic flux and enhanced precursor supply for phenylpropanoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes precursor supply and supports elevated phenylpropanoid production in tomato fruit
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to combine with regulatory engineering to boost carbon flux into phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a direct AtMYB12 target in tomato; highlighted as linking primary metabolism to aromatic amino acid synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step to aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose-4-phosphate to form DAHP
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive polyphenols with nutritional and health-promoting properties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in AtMYB12 fruit; elevated in peel of WT fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR; isotope feeding/metabolic flux analysis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter; indirectly studied under E8:AtMYB12 background
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlDAHPS is upregulated in AtMYB12 fruit. 2. AtMYB12 and SlMYB12 directly bind the SlDAHPS promoter. 3. Activation of SlDAHPS is associated with increased shikimate-pathway flux and aromatic amino acid supply for phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances entry of carbon into the shikimate pathway and supports high phenylpropanoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A valuable engineering target for increasing aromatic amino acid-derived nutritional metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly describes DAHPS as a key determinant of carbon flow into the shikimate pathway

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step from phenylalanine to cinnamate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols; chlorogenic acid; anthocyanins; resveratrol; genistein
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter; indirectly studied under E8:AtMYB12 background
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL5A, PAL5C and PAL5D promoters are directly bound by AtMYB12. 2. Their induction links AtMYB12 to activation of general phenylpropanoid metabolism. 3. Increased PAL activity contributes to elevated downstream phenylpropanoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flux from phenylalanine into multiple phenylpropanoid products
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful component for stacking with AtMYB12 and branch-pathway genes to elevate diverse phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Multiple PAL5 paralogs were enriched in ChIP-qPCR

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in AtMYB12 fruit; elevated in peel of WT fruit
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtMYB12; SlMYB12; VvStSy competes with CHS activity for substrate flux
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter; indirectly studied under E8:AtMYB12 background
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCHS1 is a direct target of AtMYB12 and SlMYB12. 2. Its activation supports strong flavonol accumulation in AtMYB12 fruit. 3. Flux through CHS can be competitively diverted by stilbene synthase during resveratrol engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonoid biosynthesis but competes with stilbene formation for shared substrate
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for tuning branch competition between flavonoids and engineered stilbenes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper highlights strong competition between VvStSy and CHS
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with stilbene branch can reduce flavonol accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin to dihydroflavonols
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonols are antioxidant phytonutrients; genistein is a phytoestrogen with nutritional relevance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in AtMYB12 fruit; elevated in peel of WT fruit
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR; qRT-PCR; mutant analysis using are
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtMYB12; SlMYB12; LjIFS competes for naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Endogenous promoter; indirectly studied under E8:AtMYB12 and E8:LjIFS backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlF3H is directly activated by AtMYB12. 2. The are mutant deficient in F3H activity lowers flavonol accumulation. 3. Reduced F3H activity strongly increases isoflavone accumulation when LjIFS is expressed, because F3H and IFS compete for naringenin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonol synthesis but limits engineered isoflavone production by competing for naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Partial suppression of F3H is effective for redirecting flux to genistein in AtMYB12-enabled tomato systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The are mutant is deficient in F3H activity

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to produce resveratrol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive stilbene with reported health-promoting and therapeutic potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; genetic crossing with E8:AtMYB12; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Competes with SlCHS1 for substrate flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 35S:VvStSy tomato accumulates about 0.5 mg g−1 DW trans-resveratrol. 2. Co-expression with AtMYB12 increases total resveratrol/resveratrol derivatives to 5-6 mg g−1 DW. 3. VvStSy competes strongly with CHS, reducing high flavonol accumulation in AtMYB12 StSy fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly enables resveratrol biosynthesis and achieves much higher accumulation when combined with AtMYB12
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for fruit biofortification with stilbenes when stacked with AtMYB12
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Polydatin increased 4-5 fold in the AtMYB12 background; methylated resveratrol derivatives also accumulated
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduces flavonol accumulation because of branch competition

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanones such as naringenin into isoflavone intermediates en route to genistein
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoestrogenic compounds of nutritional and health relevance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; crossing with E8:AtMYB12 and are mutant; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E8:LjIFS alone yields low genistin accumulation in tomato fruit. 2. Addition of AtMYB12 significantly increases genistin to about 11 mg g−1 DW. 3. Combining LjIFS with AtMYB12 and reduced F3H activity (are) raises total isoflavones to about 78 mg g−1 DW.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly enables isoflavone biosynthesis and, with AtMYB12 plus reduced F3H competition, massively enhances isoflavone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent gene for engineering high-genistein tomato when stacked with AtMYB12 and flux rerouting strategies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Genistin was the main detected product in LjIFS-only fruit; di-glucosides accumulated in stacked lines

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis and precursor supply for phenylpropanoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlENO; SlDAHPS; PAL5A; PAL5C; PAL5D; SlCHS1; SlF3H; additional likely targets include PGLS; Rpi; ALDO; SUS1; DHQD; SHD; PAT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of genes in glycolysis, oxidative pentose phosphate pathway, shikimate pathway and flavonoid biosynthesis; directly binds promoters of SlENO, SlDAHPS, PAL5A/C/D, SlCHS1 and SlF3H
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master positive regulator that controls both supply and demand, enabling exceptionally high phenylpropanoid accumulation and enhancing production of engineered stilbenes and isoflavones
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit-specific transgene expression under E8 promoter during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Binds promoter TACCTACC motif; functionally homologous to SlMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols; hydroxycinnamates/chlorogenic acid; anthocyanins; resveratrol; genistein
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary polyphenols and bioactives with antioxidant and health-promoting properties
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in fruit by E8 promoter during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; 14C/13C isotope feeding; metabolomics; genetic crosses
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible metabolite repression in photosynthetic tissues discussed; not directly demonstrated in tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. E8-driven AtMYB12 strongly induces both primary metabolism and flavonoid/phenylpropanoid genes in tomato fruit. 2. AtMYB12 directly binds promoters of SlENO, SlDAHPS, PAL5A/C/D, SlCHS1 and SlF3H. 3. AtMYB12 increases glycolytic, OPPP, TCA and shikimate-pathway flux, thereby increasing precursor, ATP and reducing-power supply. 4. AtMYB12 greatly enhances accumulation of flavonols and chlorogenic acid and boosts engineered resveratrol and isoflavone production when stacked with pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes accumulation of diverse phenylpropanoids and improves production of novel bioactive compounds in tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful engineering module for biofortification and phytochemical production; especially effective when stacked with structural genes or complementary TFs under fruit-specific expression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Constitutive 35S expression gives more modest metabolic effects and can reduce growth in tobacco; fruit-specific expression is superior
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced major sugars in fruit; possible growth penalty under constitutive expression in other species

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonol biosynthesis and associated precursor supply in tomato peel
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds promoters of SlENO, SlDAHPS and SlCHS1 and is associated with elevated expression of these genes plus SlF3H and SlFLS in peel
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Endogenous tomato homolog sharing targets with AtMYB12 and associated with peel flavonoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit tissue specificity during ripening, predominantly peel
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in peel, not flesh
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; immunodetection in WT peel/flesh comparison
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ripening WT fruit at 5 days after breaker
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB12 is predominantly expressed in tomato peel. 2. SlMYB12 binds the same promoter motifs in SlENO, SlDAHPS and SlCHS1 as AtMYB12. 3. SlMYB12 is associated with elevated expression of primary- and flavonoid-pathway genes in peel, supporting endogenous flavonol accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes peel-associated flavonoid accumulation and precursor-supply gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Endogenous regulatory target for enhancing peel flavonoids in tomato breeding
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented mainly as functional tomato homolog validating AtMYB12 target conservation, but experimentally analyzed in Results

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bHLH transcription factor (Delila); R2R3-MYB transcription factor (Rosea1)
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes encoding enzymes late in flavonoid/anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Mainly activate anthocyanin biosynthetic genes; do not significantly activate primary metabolic pathways
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulators that enhance anthocyanin accumulation but are less broad than AtMYB12 because they mainly control demand, not supply
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Transgenic expression in purple tomato line
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely MYB-bHLH regulatory module, but direct complex evidence not detailed here
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenically expressed in purple fruit line
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic comparison/crossing with AtMYB12; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Delila and Rosea1 primarily activate anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in tomato fruit. 2. They do not substantially induce primary metabolism or aromatic amino acid accumulation. 3. Crossing with AtMYB12 creates Indigo fruit with higher anthocyanins, flavonols and chlorogenic acid than parental lines.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increase anthocyanin accumulation; synergize with AtMYB12 for broader phenylpropanoid enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful TFs for stacking with AtMYB12 to maximize fruit anthocyanin and total phenylpropanoid content
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as combined experimental module rather than individually dissected in this paper
